title: Tapping Into Fun on National Tap Dance Day
story: Dancers across America lace up their special metal-tipped shoes and hit the floor with clicks, clacks, and rhythmic beats on May 25 for National Tap Dance Day! Dance studios throw open their doors for free lessons, while performers take to the streets and stages to show off their fancy footwork. You'll see everything from beginners learning basic shuffles to pros creating lightning-fast rhythms that sound like drums. Social media fills with videos of people tap dancing everywhere – on sidewalks, in kitchens, even on rooftops!
activity_question: What makes tap dance shoes create their distinctive sounds?
activity_choices: Metal tips attached to the bottom
activity_answer: Metal tips attached to the bottom
follow_up_question: What everyday sounds around you could inspire a tap dance rhythm?

title: Don't Panic! It's Towel Day
story: Every May 25, fans around the world celebrate Towel Day by carrying towels everywhere they go! This quirky holiday honors Douglas Adams, the brilliant author who wrote "The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy." In his famous books, towels are the most useful thing any space traveler can own. On this special day, people bring towels to work, school, restaurants, and even grocery stores. They post photos online showing off their favorite towels and share quotes from Adams' hilarious stories. Some fans organize towel-themed parties or read passages from the books together. It's a wonderfully weird way to remember a writer who made millions of people laugh!
activity_question: According to the story, what do people do with towels on Towel Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual place you'd bring a towel to celebrate this day?

title: Geek Pride Day: Nerds Rule the World!
story: Every May 25, geeks everywhere celebrate being proudly nerdy! Geek Pride Day is when people show off their love for science fiction, fantasy books, video games, comics, and all things tech. Fans wear their favorite superhero t-shirts, post about their latest gaming achievements, and share photos of their comic book collections. Some organize trivia contests about Star Wars or Harry Potter. Others host board game marathons or coding workshops. The day encourages everyone to embrace their inner geek without feeling embarrassed. Whether you're obsessed with robots, dragons, or space exploration, this holiday says being passionate about what you love is totally awesome!
activity_question: What do people typically do on Geek Pride Day?
activity_choices: Show off their love for comics, games, and sci-fi
Hide their nerdy interests from friends
Avoid all technology for the day
activity_answer: Show off their love for comics, games, and sci-fi
follow_up_question: What geeky hobby or interest would you be most excited to celebrate?

title: Helping Heroes Find Their Way Home
story: Every May 25, communities across America come together for National Missing Children's Day to help keep kids safe and bring missing children home to their families. Schools teach students about staying safe, while parents review important safety rules with their kids. Police departments host special events where families can get ID cards made with their children's photos and fingerprints. Many people share photos and information about missing children on social media to help spread awareness. It's a day when everyone works together like detectives to protect children and help families reunite.
activity_question: What do police departments often help families create on National Missing Children's Day?
activity_choices: ID cards with photos and fingerprints
activity_answer: ID cards with photos and fingerprints
follow_up_question: What safety rules do you think are most important for kids to remember?
